title: Chapter Twenty-One: The Hidden Dragon
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

"As I mentioned when assigning the Blood Flame Cult case, Mount Hua Sect lost their Great Violet Pill."

Ma Jin sat with his hands on his knees, the scar pulling tight across his face. It was early, and the morning light through the window made the scarred tissue look like old parchment.

"The Shaanxi branch said they had no resources because of it," Yeon-shin said. "That was why I ended up facing the Blood Swordmaster alone."

"Correct. The pill is still unresolved. It's the ultimate medicinal treasure of the martial world -- worth more than the yearly revenues of a minor sect. Every faction in Shaanxi has become involved. Sects eager to impress Mount Hua by interrogating suspects. Martial artists settling old scores under the guise of investigation. The entire province is in chaos."

Yeon-shin listened. His face was not serious.

The entire Shaanxi martial world, vaster than small countries, was embroiled in conflict. Swordsmen claiming to practise justice were spread throughout the province.

*All of them potential sparring partners. All of them carrying martial arts that could refine the Eternal Blossom Fist.*

Perhaps his excitement showed on his face. Ma Jin shifted with visible discomfort.

"You'll accompany Mount Hua Sect. Join their search for the Great Violet Pill until the conflict ends."

"Another sect's martial artist helping find their pill?"

"Partly surveillance. Mount Hua knows this. They've already fought a major battle with unorthodox sects -- collapsed an entire inn in Xi'an, the largest in the city. Massive casualties. Radiant Demon Wing's role is to moderate their use of force at the centre of this storm."

"Will Mount Hua accept it?"

"They want to become the second Shaolin Temple. Imperial recognition as the representative of the Taoist sects. Even if you went alone, you'd be treated well enough. Though there would be wariness."

Yeon-shin remained silent for a moment. Then spoke.

"Large conflicts don't often occur in the martial world. With proper justification, it would be a place where I can freely wield my sword in real combat."

"Given your qualities, I think you can achieve incomparable growth. An opportunity to freely exchange swords with all sorts of renowned martial artists under the name of Desolate Sect."

He had already agreed internally. Martial arts without real combat experience were no different from theory. The Eternal Blossom Fist was a newly created technique lacking in forms, and this mission could help fill those gaps. There would not be many other chances to observe the martial arts of the Nine Schools.

"You've already completed one mission excellently. This time it's at your discretion."

"I'll go."

He did not think too hard about it. He also felt he should distance himself from the Desolate Ma family. He did not know how his grandfather with his madness would try to persuade him. The moment they exchanged something as blood relatives, they would become entangled. It could hold him back when he needed to move forward.

*Travel outside as much as possible. No rest periods. Build achievements.*

"What about personnel?"

"Same as the Blood Flame mission. Heon Won-chang is new, and Cheong Myeong and Baek Mi-ryeo's punishment from the Azure Sky Squad incident hasn't ended. They volunteered eagerly." A pause. "I'll be going too."

"You, Master?"

"When dealing with the Nine Sects, someone of the Divine Sword Squad leader's level should go to match their status."

"When do we depart?"

"Tomorrow. Pack your bags as soon as you return."

Yeon-shin nodded slowly. Learning martial arts from the Sect Leader would have to wait until after returning. Now was the time to master the Eternal Blossom Fist. The experience from this mission would be a great help.

---

Plum blossoms fell like pink snow through the sunlight.

Xi'an spread before them, its streets wide and elegant with the refined charm of a city that had prospered since before the founding of the nation. Unlike Xiangyang, which brimmed with merchant groups and peddlers, this place had a slower dignity -- old stone and careful craftsmanship.

*I hope the next mission is somewhere with post stations.*

The time lost to travel gnawed at him. The rows of fully bloomed plum blossoms felt oppressive now. Time simply refused to stop.

"What a shame," Heon Won-chang grumbled. "The centre of Shaanxi's martial world, and we can't wear Desolate Sect's robes."

They wore ordinary clothes for Mount Hua Sect's dignity. Yeon-shin had grown used to playing along with Won-chang's idle chatter.

"It doesn't seem as prosperous as present-day Xiangyang."

"Xi'an is where all sorts of masters interact. Even excluding Mount Hua and Zhongnan."

Ma Jin dismounted before a massive inn said to have two hundred years of tradition. Its appearance was almost like a tavern. When passing through cities, they never stayed at shabby places. Such was Desolate Sect's wealth.

"Xi'an is Zhongnan Sect's territory," Ma Jin said. "It's not a place where Mount Hua can act freely. We need to be careful too."

That was when the inn's doors opened.

Yeon-shin felt a cluster of intense energy -- sharp, unified, like a single blade composed of many edges. Male and female Taoist priests filed out in perfect formation. Over a dozen, but it did not feel crowded.

The approaching people felt like one giant sword.

Most noticeable were the plum blossom embroideries on the hems of everyone's white martial robes.

*Mount Hua Sect.*

The middle-aged swordsman at the front emanated a formidable aura that matched his sharp appearance. Comparable to the first time Yeon-shin had met Ma Jin.

"So the masters from Desolate Sect have arrived. We've been waiting."

"Taoist Cheon Ju," Ma Jin said, giving a brief martial salute. "The road was difficult, so we barely managed to match the timing."

Yeon-shin felt Heon Won-chang start beside him. At least the man had the sense not to speak.

*Leader of the Plum Blossom Swordsmen. Mount Hua Sword Saint Cheon Ju.*

His name reached beyond Shaanxi Province. The supreme master who led Mount Hua Sect's elite swordsmen had personally descended the mountain.

"Radiant Demon Wing. Bewitching Sword Demon. Blue-eyed Demon Sword," Cheon Ju said, scanning them with an expressionless face. "All familiar faces."

*Demon Sword?*

Blue-eyed Demon Sword Cheong Myeong. It was his first time hearing this title. How did that languid Elf fight against enemies? In Jinpyeong County, he had only seen him exchange swords briefly with the Blood Flame Cult. He could not help but be curious.

Cheon Ju's gaze settled on Yeon-shin.

"I can see how Desolate Sect views this matter, bringing even a disciple of Radiant Demon Wing."

The sword-like gaze seemed to pierce through him. He let it pass indifferently. He had just faced the Desolate Sect Leader and the former Divine Sword Squad Leader. Though the Mount Hua Sword Saint was a great master, he did not match up to them.

Then Yeon-shin noticed a young swordsman among the Mount Hua masters. Languid expression. He even yawned slightly, as if everything in the world was trivial. When their eyes met, the young Taoist smirked and looked up at the sky.

"That's--" Ma Jin began.

"That Desolate Sect disciple seems quite extraordinary," Cheon Ju interrupted, turning back to Ma Jin. "How about having him exchange pointers with our Sect Leader's disciple? We've acknowledged each other, but the companionship may be uncomfortable for both sides. It could serve as a small entertainment."

*They want to break our spirit.*

Mount Hua knew this accompaniment was surveillance. They were one of the top sword sects in the world. Even accepting due to the imperial court, they could not help feeling resentful. Since it would be awkward for the main masters to cross swords when they had to travel together, they wanted to determine superiority through their young.

Yeon-shin tapped the sword guard of the Desolate Sword at his waist.

Ma Jin looked at him.

"My master has brought trouble again," the young Taoist priest called out, languid as a cat. "Is five moves alright?"

He had not even bothered to lower his voice.

"There will be things to learn," Cheon Ju said. "Give it your all."

"What is there to learn? I heard Radiant Demon Wing's martial arts are about sensation."

The youth stepped forward with a sigh, and the area in front of the inn became an impromptu arena. Passersby who had been peeking began excitedly turning into spectators. Whispers about Mount Hua Sect came from all directions.

"The hidden dragon of Mount Hua!"

"I heard the Sect Leader's disciple was an unparalleled genius!"

"They say he's mastered the Falling Petal Palm and Six Harmonies Divine Sword."

Yeon-shin laughed internally. The two faced each other.

"You mentioned five moves," Yeon-shin said.

"Are you offended? Sorry. Morning practice was just tiring." The young Taoist priest gave a vague smile. "I'm Yu Hyeon of Mount Hua Sect."

"Jeong Yeon-shin."

In plain clothes, he could not mention Radiant Demon Wing. Voices from the crowd called it discourteous for a student to give only his name.

When two martial artists face each other, they speak only through martial arts.

Yu Hyeon's body silently grew larger. He rushed in with footwork resembling a noble's gait -- the famous Drifting Dark Fragrance of Mount Hua. His raised hand struck down, the widely spread palm stretching out like a flower pattern.

*The Falling Petal Palm. Not superfluous, but with many variations. To prevent breaking down the forms.*

His heart was dyed with interest.

He deflected with the back of his clenched fist. The colliding palm force was light.

Yeon-shin's force extended without losing power, enough to drive into a whirlwind. The first form of the Eternal Blossom Fist.

Spiral.

The impact tore across the distance between them. Yu Hyeon's face filled with bewilderment as the passing force whirled him sideways. Just from the residual energy.

"What--!"

If Yeon-shin had Yu Hyeon's level of internal energy, that single strike would have broken his neck.

Now it was his turn to advance. One step forward. A straight punch from behind his waist, like a steel arrow leaving the bowstring. The second form, Advancing Thunder -- footwork bringing thunder from his fist.

The ground overturned from the scattered force fragments. Dust hazed the sunlight.

Yu Hyeon, now covered in grime, dispersed the force's aftermath with dazzling hand movements -- the Falling Petal Palm again, used defensively. Yeon-shin studied the flowing energy pattern.

*Sufficient to inspire the third form.*

He spoke with a pleased smile.

"A bit longer. I'd like to go for about ten moves."

Silence from the crowd. And from Yu Hyeon -- nothing at all.

---
